set_gear.brief: A structure that represents a gear set and its associated bonus effects based on the number of pieces equipped.
set_gear.details: The set_gear struct models a gear set within the game, encapsulating its unique identifier, display name, and the various bonus effects activated by equipping multiple pieces from the set. The set_name member is an integer that uniquely identifies the specific gear set, facilitating efficient management and referencing within the game system. The display member provides a human-readable name or description of the set, used for user interface presentation. The set1 through set5 members are strings that specify the effects or identifiers for bonuses granted when the player equips 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 pieces of the set, respectively. This design allows for flexible and scalable management of set bonuses, supporting dynamic application and display of effects based on the current equipment configuration. The structure integrates with other game systems to ensure consistent bonus application and user feedback regarding gear sets.

tparams.set_name: Unique identifier for the gear set, used to differentiate and manage specific sets.
tparams.display: User-facing name or description of the gear set displayed to players.
tparams.set1: Bonus effect or identifier activated when one piece of the set is equipped.
tparams.set2: Bonus effect or identifier activated when two pieces of the set are equipped.
tparams.set3: Bonus effect or identifier activated when three pieces of the set are equipped.
tparams.set4: Bonus effect or identifier activated when four pieces of the set are equipped.
tparams.set5: Bonus effect or identifier activated when five pieces of the set are equipped.

set_gear.notes: This struct employs a straightforward approach to managing gear set bonuses, with each bonus effect stored as a string identifier. This allows for flexible assignment and easy extension of bonus effects without altering the core structure. The design supports dynamic bonus application based on the number of set pieces equipped, enabling scalable and maintainable game mechanics.
set_gear.rationale: The set_gear struct exists to organize and manage gear set information systematically, enabling the game to recognize, display, and apply set bonuses efficiently. By encapsulating set identifiers, display names, and bonus effects within a single structure, it facilitates consistent handling of gear sets across various game systems, such as equipment management, UI updates, and effect application. This design supports modularity and scalability, making it easier to add new sets or modify existing ones as the game evolves.
set_gear.name: set_gear

set_name.brief: An integer representing the specific set name identifier used in the game.
set_name.details: The variable `set_name` stores an integer value that uniquely identifies a particular set, such as `SET_MAGE_INVOKER`. It is initialized with predefined constants corresponding to different set names and is used throughout the game logic to reference, validate, and manage sets. This variable facilitates operations like displaying set information, applying set effects, and ensuring consistency in set-related functionalities.
set_name.notes: Typically, `set_name` is assigned a constant value from a set of predefined identifiers. Proper management of these constants ensures correct recognition and handling of different sets within the game system.
set_name.rationale: This variable exists to provide a straightforward way to identify and differentiate between various predefined sets, enabling consistent set management and effect application throughout the game.
set_name.name: set_name

display.brief: A string representing the gear or equipment state displayed to the player.
display.details: The `display` variable stores the text shown to the player, indicating the current gear or equipment configuration. It is initialized with a descriptive string and can be updated dynamically in response to game events or user interactions. Utilizing the custom `String` class, it benefits from enhanced string manipulation features, such as case-insensitive comparisons, which aid in flexible display updates and logic checks related to gear status.
display.notes: The `display` string is typically managed within the `set_gear` context to reflect real-time changes in gear state, ensuring the player receives accurate visual feedback.
display.rationale: This variable exists to provide a clear and adaptable visual representation of the gear status, supporting user interface updates and game state consistency.
display.name: display

set1.brief: A string that stores the identifier for the first set bonus applied to a character or item.
set1.details: The `set1` variable holds the identifier or description of the first set bonus currently active. It is initialized with a string value that specifies the particular bonus, which can be used in game logic to apply effects, trigger events, or display information to the player. The variable utilizes the custom `String` class, enabling advanced string manipulation and case-insensitive comparisons, thereby simplifying bonus management and ensuring consistency across different parts of the codebase.
set1.notes: Since `set1` is stored as a `String` object, it benefits from extended string functionalities, which can be useful for flexible bonus identification and comparison operations.
set1.rationale: This variable exists to track which specific set bonus is active in the first slot, supporting game mechanics that depend on set bonuses and ensuring that the correct effects are applied based on the current bonus configuration.
set1.name: set1

set2.brief: A string that stores information related to the second piece of a gear set bonus.
set2.details: The variable `set2` holds descriptive information or status details pertaining to the second gear piece in a set bonus configuration. It is initialized as an empty string and can be updated dynamically based on game events, player equipment, or set activation conditions. The `String` class used provides extended string functionalities, such as case-insensitive comparisons and advanced manipulation, facilitating flexible handling of set bonus identifiers or descriptions within the game's logic.
set2.notes: Utilizing the `String` class allows for more robust string operations, which can be beneficial when matching set bonus names or statuses regardless of case variations.
set2.rationale: This variable exists to track and manage the state or description of the second gear set bonus, supporting game mechanics that activate or modify bonuses based on equipment configurations.
set2.name: set2

set3.brief: Stores the identifier or description of the third gear piece that grants a set bonus.
set3.details: The variable 'set3' holds information about the third piece in a gear set, typically including its name or identifier. It is initialized with data corresponding to the specific gear item and is used in logic that verifies set completion or applies set bonuses. The String class allows for efficient string operations, including case-insensitive comparisons, which aids in matching gear pieces regardless of naming conventions. This variable plays a key role in managing and applying set bonus effects based on equipped gear pieces.
set3.notes: Utilizes a custom String class to enable advanced string handling, ensuring reliable matching of gear piece identifiers regardless of case or formatting.
set3.rationale: This variable exists to track the third gear piece in a set, enabling the system to recognize when a complete set is equipped and to apply the corresponding bonus effects accordingly.
set3.name: set3

set4.brief: A string that stores the description or identifier of the 4-piece bonus set effect.
set4.details: The variable 'set4' holds information about the 4-piece bonus activated when a character equips a complete gear set. It is initialized with the specific bonus details, which may include effect descriptions or identifiers used for display and logic processing. The String class provides enhanced string manipulation capabilities, ensuring reliable handling of set bonus data across various operations such as comparisons and formatting. This variable is integral to applying set effects correctly within the game's calculation and UI systems.
set4.notes: Utilizes the custom String class for flexible and case-insensitive string operations, ensuring consistent handling of set bonus information.
set4.rationale: This variable exists to track and represent the active 4-piece bonus effect, enabling the system to apply the corresponding effects and display relevant information to the player.
set4.name: set4

set5.brief: A string that stores the description or identifier of the 5-piece bonus set effect.
set5.details: The `set5` variable holds the identifier or descriptive text for the 5-piece bonus effect granted by a gear set. It is initialized with a string value that specifies the particular bonus activated when five items from the set are equipped. This variable is used in set bonus calculations, display routines, and effect checks throughout the codebase to determine whether the 5-piece bonus is active and to present relevant information to the user.
set5.notes: Utilizes the custom String class, which provides case-insensitive comparisons and advanced string manipulation, ensuring flexible handling of set bonus identifiers.
set5.rationale: This variable exists to represent and manage the 5-piece bonus effect associated with gear sets, enabling consistent checking, display, and application of set bonuses within the game logic.
set5.name: set5
